subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: every technology has an inherent bias. It has within its physical form a predisposition toward being used in certain ways and not others.
rationale: A medium's form is a fixed predisposition baked into the substrate — the book's 'medium is frozen selection' claim.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: Each technology has an agenda of its own. It is, as I have suggested, a metaphor waiting to unfold.
rationale: A medium carries its own criterial agenda shaping what survives downstream — supports selection as a criterial mechanism.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: how television stages the world becomes the model for how the world is properly to be staged... off the screen the same metaphor prevails.
rationale: The dominant medium's logic propagates to every other institution and gate, even off-screen — a medium attached to every gate.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: television demands a different kind of content from other media. You cannot do political philosophy on television. Its form works against the content.
rationale: The medium bounds the option space — defines which variants are viable to express — the 'medium bounds' operation.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: You cannot use smoke to do philosophy. Its form excludes the content.
rationale: A medium pre-empts content by excluding it from the expressible forms — form-excludes-content is the option-space bounded.

subject: concept:option-space
quote: It is in the nature of the medium that it must suppress the content of ideas in order to accommodate the requirements of visual interest
rationale: Postman develops the medium as suppressing which viable variants an idea can take at a stage — the option-space concept.

subject: mechanism:medium-shapes-want
quote: television has achieved the status of "meta-medium"—an instrument that directs not only our knowledge of the world, but our knowledge of ways of knowing as well.
rationale: The medium shapes the receiver's whole epistemology and appetite, not just content — the medium-shapes-want mechanism.

subject: question:medium-and-manipulation
quote: a technology is to a medium as the brain is to the mind... A technology, in other words, is merely a machine. A medium is the social and intellectual environment a machine creates.
rationale: Postman's technology/medium distinction operationalizes what the medium does to discourse — a definitional contribution.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: If all of this sounds suspiciously like Marshall McLuhan's aphorism, the medium is the message, I will not disavow the association
rationale: Postman situates his medium argument as a development of McLuhan's Understanding Media — direct intellectual lineage.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: His aphorism, however, is in need of amendment because, as it stands, it may lead one to confuse a message with a metaphor.
rationale: Postman amends McLuhan: the medium is a metaphor working by implication, not a message — refines a flat 'medium shapes gates' reading.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: They must follow where their medium leads. There is no conspiracy here, no lack of intelligence, only a straightforward recognition that "good television" has little to do with what is "good" about exposition
rationale: Postman locates harm in the medium's intrinsic form, not in tunable gate criteria — pressure-tests selection as the locus of repair.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: in answering the question, What is television?, we must understand as a first point that we are not talking about television as a technology but television as a medium.
rationale: Postman treats the medium as irreducible fate, not a tunable choice — complicates the frozen-medium-to-adjustable-gate continuum.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: The problem is not that television presents us with entertaining subject matter but that all subject matter is presented as entertaining
rationale: Postman roots the breakage in the medium's blanket entertainment form, not in gates rewarding specific truth-losing compressions.

subject: concept:memetic-fitness
quote: Entertainment is the supra-ideology of all discourse on television. No matter what is depicted or from what point of view, the overarching presumption is that it is there for our amusement and pleasure.
rationale: Postman names one dominant fitness criterion the medium imposes on all content — entertainment value — the gate's selection criteria.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: bite-sized is best, that complexity must be avoided, that nuances are dispensable, that qualifications impede the simple message, that visual stimulation is a substitute for thought
rationale: The medium's gate systematically rewards compressions that strip nuance and qualification — support for gates rewarding truth loss.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: we are presented not only with fragmented news but news without context, without consequences, without value, and therefore without essential seriousness; that is to say, news as pure entertainment.
rationale: Compression strips context (the decoding key / outer message) while the bare claim travels on — news-without-context is outer-message loss.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: Meaning is distorted when a word or sentence is, as we say, taken out of context; when a reader or listener is deprived of what was said before, and after.
rationale: A claim needs surrounding context to decode correctly — context-deprivation maps to the outer-message-as-preconditions claim.

subject: claim:compression-widens-latitude
quote: Disappear the context, or fragment it, and contradiction disappears... In the context of no context, so to speak, it simply disappears.
rationale: Stripping context unpins a claim so it can't be checked against its opposite — contradiction-disappears is latitude widened by compression.

subject: concept:truth-compression-regimes
quote: television provides a new (or, possibly, restores an old) definition of truth: The credibility of the teller is the ultimate test of the truth of a proposition.
rationale: A medium can reroute a claim's truth value to an orthogonal axis (the teller's charm) — the orthogonality regime.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: the concept of truth is intimately linked to the biases of forms of expression. Truth does not, and never has, come unadorned. It must appear in its proper clothing or it is not acknowledged
rationale: Truth survives only if the medium's form rewards something correlated with truth — supports the first survival condition.

subject: claim:truth-value-is-key-fit
quote: the weight assigned to any form of truth-telling is a function of the influence of media of communication.
rationale: Truth value depends on the medium-supplied decoding frame, not the bare claim — engages truth-value-is-key-fit from the media side.

subject: question:truth-value-placement
quote: when news is packaged as entertainment, that is the inevitable result... we are losing our sense of what it means to be well informed.
rationale: Postman locates where truth degrades — not false claims but misleading fragments — engaging where truth value enters the pipeline.

subject: mechanism:transport
quote: the telegraph... made it possible to move decontextualized information over vast spaces at incredible speed.
rationale: Transit itself strips context as information moves across distance — an account of the transport mechanism's decontextualizing effect.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: television offers viewers a variety of subject matter, requires minimal skills to comprehend it, and is largely aimed at emotional gratification.
rationale: Low-precondition emotional content spreads, but Postman credits the medium's form not transit — complicates a pure-transport reading.

subject: source:amusing-ourselves-to-death
quote: For no medium is excessively dangerous if its users understand what its dangers are.
rationale: Postman frames the medium itself as the dangerous surface, made safe only by demystification — touches manipulation-surface.

_note: Unanchored concepts Postman develops: technology/medium distinction (machine vs. environment it creates); 'pseudo-context' (a fake use for decontextualized info); Orwell/Huxley axis (control by pain vs. pleasure). He also declines to claim media alter cognition.
